_note: IEC 60364 is the international parent standard for low-voltage electrical installations. National bodies adopt it with local amendments: BS 7671 (UK), HD 60364 (CENELEC/Europe), NF C 15-100 (France), DIN VDE 0100 (Germany), NEN 1010 (Netherlands), NIS 197 (Nigeria) etc.

IEC_60364_1.title: Part 1 — Fundamental principles, assessment of general characteristics, definitions
IEC_60364_1.scope: Fundamental safety objectives, scope of the standard, assessment of general characteristics of an installation (supply characteristics, external influences, cross-sectional area of conductors, identification of conductors)

IEC_60364_4_41.title: Part 4-41 — Protection for safety — Protection against electric shock

amendments.summary: Added requirements for socket outlets with increased risk, updated disconnection times Table 41.1, clarified PELV requirements

IEC_60364_4_41.scope: Automatic disconnection of supply (ADS), SELV, PELV, FELV, Class II equipment, earth-free local equipotential bonding, electrical separation

IEC_60364_4_42.title: Part 4-42 — Protection for safety — Protection against thermal effects
IEC_60364_4_42.scope: Protection against fire, ignition of materials, burns from equipment surfaces, overheating of insulation. Covers requirements for wiring systems in fire-risk locations.

IEC_60364_4_43.title: Part 4-43 — Protection for safety — Protection against overcurrent
IEC_60364_4_43.scope: Protection against overload current and short-circuit current. Fundamental Rule: Ib ≤ In ≤ Iz. Placement of protective devices. Back-up protection.

IEC_60364_4_44.title: Part 4-44 — Protection for safety — Protection against voltage disturbances and electromagnetic disturbances

amendments.summary: Overvoltage categories updated, SPD coordination requirements added
Expanded mandatory SPD installation scope. SPDs now required for all new installations unless risk analysis justifies omission. Added requirements for photovoltaic systems.

IEC_60364_4_44.scope: Overvoltages of atmospheric origin (lightning), overvoltages from network faults, switching overvoltages. SPD selection, coordination, and installation.

IEC_60364_5_51.title: Part 5-51 — Selection and erection of electrical equipment — Common rules
IEC_60364_5_51.scope: Common rules for selection and erection of all electrical equipment. Compliance with standards, external influences (AE, AF, AG, etc.), accessibility, identification.

IEC_60364_5_52.title: Part 5-52 — Selection and erection of electrical equipment — Wiring systems

amendments.summary: Updated cable rating tables (Annex B), new harmonic correction factors (Annex E), updated grouping factors, added Method F for single-core cables flat touching
Further updates to cable rating tables for XLPE cables, updated soil thermal resistivity correction, EV charging wiring requirements

IEC_60364_5_52.status: current — verify AMD2 adoption in your jurisdiction
IEC_60364_5_52.scope: Wiring systems — types of wiring, installation methods, current-carrying capacities, voltage drop, selection and erection requirements. The primary source for cable sizing data.

IEC_60364_5_53.title: Part 5-53 — Selection and erection of electrical equipment — Devices for protection, isolation, switching, control and monitoring
IEC_60364_5_53.scope: Selection and coordination of protective devices (MCBs, RCDs, fuses, isolators, switches). Functional switching, emergency stop, monitoring devices.

IEC_60364_5_54.title: Part 5-54 — Selection and erection of electrical equipment — Earthing arrangements, protective conductors and protective bonding conductors

amendments.summary: Updated electrode sizing, added requirements for combined PEN conductors in TN-C systems, soil resistivity guidance updated

IEC_60364_5_54.scope: Earthing systems (TN, TT, IT), protective conductors (CPCs), main and supplementary equipotential bonding, earthing electrodes, earth electrode resistance requirements.

IEC_60364_5_55.title: Part 5-55 — Selection and erection of electrical equipment — Other equipment
IEC_60364_5_55.scope: Luminaires and lighting installations, low-voltage generating sets, UPS systems, safety services, ELV lighting systems, photovoltaic source circuits.

IEC_60364_5_56.title: Part 5-56 — Selection and erection of electrical equipment — Safety services
IEC_60364_5_56.scope: Electrical installations for safety services — fire alarm, emergency lighting, smoke extraction, fire suppression. Source selection, standby power, monitoring.

IEC_60364_6.title: Part 6 — Verification

amendments.summary: Updated test voltage levels, added periodic inspection intervals guidance, added requirements for EV charging point verification

IEC_60364_6.scope: Initial verification (inspection + testing) and periodic inspection. Test methods: insulation resistance, polarity, earth fault loop impedance, RCD, continuity. Documentation.

IEC_60364_7_701.title: Part 7-701 — Special locations — Rooms containing a bath or shower
IEC_60364_7_701.scope: Zone definitions (0, 1, 2), IP requirements, prohibited and permitted equipment in each zone, supplementary bonding.

IEC_60364_7_702.title: Part 7-702 — Special locations — Swimming pools and fountains
IEC_60364_7_702.scope: Zone definitions for pools and fountains, IP requirements, FELV/SELV requirements, fixed equipment restrictions.

IEC_60364_7_703.title: Part 7-703 — Special locations — Rooms and cabins for sauna heaters
IEC_60364_7_703.scope: Zone-based requirements for sauna rooms. High temperature requirements. IP ratings. Wiring systems resistant to heat.

IEC_60364_7_704.title: Part 7-704 — Special locations — Construction and demolition site installations
IEC_60364_7_704.scope: Temporary installations on construction sites. IP requirements, RCD requirements, supply arrangements, portable tools.

IEC_60364_7_705.title: Part 7-705 — Special locations — Electrical installations of agricultural and horticultural premises
IEC_60364_7_705.scope: Agricultural buildings — livestock, irrigation, corrosive atmospheres. RCD requirements, IP ratings, bonding of metallic structures.

IEC_60364_7_708.title: Part 7-708 — Special locations — Marinas and pleasure craft
IEC_60364_7_708.scope: Shore supply connections to boats, pedestal requirements, RCD requirements, corrosion protection.

IEC_60364_7_710.title: Part 7-710 — Special locations — Medical locations
IEC_60364_7_710.scope: Medical locations Group 0, 1, 2. IT system requirements for Group 2. Isolated power supply systems (IPS). Medical IT monitoring.

IEC_60364_7_711.title: Part 7-711 — Special locations — Exhibitions, shows and stands
IEC_60364_7_711.scope: Temporary electrical installations for exhibitions. Protective measures, RCD requirements, IP requirements for temporary structures.

IEC_60364_7_712.title: Part 7-712 — Special locations — Solar photovoltaic (PV) power supply systems
IEC_60364_7_712.scope: DC wiring, protection against reverse current, PV array design, inverter connection, earthing of PV systems, string protection, monitoring.

IEC_60364_7_714.title: Part 7-714 — Special locations — External lighting installations
IEC_60364_7_714.scope: Street lighting, facade lighting, garden and landscape lighting. IP requirements, protection against electric shock, RCD requirements for street furniture.

IEC_60364_7_715.title: Part 7-715 — Special locations — Extra-low voltage lighting installations
IEC_60364_7_715.scope: ELV lighting tracks, lamp holders, transformers. Current limitations, protective bonding, thermal requirements.

IEC_60364_7_717.title: Part 7-717 — Special locations — Mobile or transportable units
IEC_60364_7_717.scope: Electrical installations in mobile or transportable units (containers, vehicles used as premises). Protective measures for connection to fixed supply.

IEC_60364_7_721.title: Part 7-721 — Special locations — Electrical installations in caravans and motor caravans
IEC_60364_7_721.scope: Permanent wiring in caravans, connection inlets, protection against overcurrent and indirect contact.

IEC_60364_7_722.title: Part 7-722 — Special locations — Supply of electric vehicles
IEC_60364_7_722.status: current — Amendment in preparation for V2G
IEC_60364_7_722.scope: EV charging equipment (Mode 1–4), protective measures, RCD requirements (30mA type A or B for Mode 2/3), cable sizing for EV loads, load management.

IEC_60364_7_740.title: Part 7-740 — Special locations — Temporary electrical installations for structures, amusement devices and booths at fairgrounds, amusement parks and circuses
IEC_60364_7_740.scope: Temporary installations for fairgrounds and amusements. RCD requirements, IP requirements, mechanical protection.

IEC_60364_7_753.title: Part 7-753 — Special locations — Floor and ceiling heating systems
IEC_60364_7_753.scope: Electric floor and ceiling heating cables and foils. SELV requirements, RCD requirements, protective mesh, zone restrictions.

key_concepts.U0: Nominal line-to-earth voltage (230V for most 230/400V systems)
key_concepts.note: IEC 60364 uses 230V as the reference voltage for most calculations. Countries on 220V or 240V nominal should verify which tables apply.

relationship_to_national_standards.BS_7671: UK national implementation — derived from IEC 60364 with UK-specific additions. HD 60364 series (CENELEC) is the European harmonised version.
relationship_to_national_standards.HD_60364: CENELEC harmonisation document — European version, minor national deviations allowed
relationship_to_national_standards.NF_C_15_100: French national version — more prescriptive in some areas, socket outlet requirements differ
relationship_to_national_standards.DIN_VDE_0100: German national version — closely aligned with IEC, stricter in some industrial applications
relationship_to_national_standards.AS_NZS_3000: Australia/New Zealand Wiring Rules — IEC 60364 aligned but with specific differences for 230V/50Hz system at 240V nominal
relationship_to_national_standards.SANS_10142: South Africa — IEC 60364 based with local earthing and supply deviations
relationship_to_national_standards.NIS_197: Nigeria — IEC 60364 based, adopted by Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON)
